name: repos
context: Create, clone, configure, and manage GitHub repositories via the API
info: This skill covers the full repository lifecycle on GitHub: creating new repos (public or private, user or org-owned), cloning and forking existing repos, configuring settings like default branches and merge options, and managing branch protection rules. All operations use the GitHub REST API via @octokit/rest. Agents use this skill to bootstrap new projects, fork upstream repos for contribution, and enforce branch policies. See [[github/auth]] for authentication setup and [[github/webhooks]] for event-driven repo automation.

sections.title: Creating repositories
sections.body: Use `octokit.repos.createForAuthenticatedUser()` for personal repos and `octokit.repos.createInOrg()` for organization repos. Set `auto_init: true` to create with a default README. Set `private: true` for private repos. The response includes `html_url`, `clone_url`, and `default_branch`. See [[github/auth]] for token setup.

sections.title: Cloning and forking
sections.body: Clone via HTTPS (`https://github.com/owner/repo.git`) or SSH (`git@github.com:owner/repo.git`). Fork with `octokit.repos.createFork()`. Forks are the standard contribution model — fork, branch, commit, then open a [[github/pull-requests]] back to the upstream repo.

sections.title: Repository settings
sections.body: Configure with `octokit.repos.update()`. Key settings: `default_branch`, `has_issues`, `has_wiki`, `allow_squash_merge`, `allow_merge_commit`, `allow_rebase_merge`, `delete_branch_on_merge`. These control how [[github/pull-requests]] are merged and what features are enabled.

sections.title: Branch protection rules
sections.body: Protect branches with `octokit.repos.updateBranchProtection()`. Require status checks from [[github/actions]] workflows, enforce pull request reviews, restrict who can push, and require signed commits. Branch protection ensures code quality by gating merges on passing CI and peer review.
